system.name: SmartReno AI Agent System
system.goal: Use AI agents to assist homeowners, estimators, contractors, subs, PMs, and admins across the existing SmartReno workflows.

orchestrator.name: Core Agent Orchestrator
orchestrator.role: Route requests to the right agent, share context (project, user, messages), and coordinate multi-step flows.
key_responsibilities: Look at incoming request (who, where, why) and choose the right agent.
Pass in relevant project/user data from Supabase and existing APIs.
Allow humans to override or approve key steps (estimates, bids, messages).
Log all AI activity for debugging and audit.

categories.name: Homeowner Agents
categories.description: Agents that interact with homeowners inside the existing Homeowner Portal.

agents.name: Smart Intake Agent
where_it_runs: Homeowner intake flow
Project creation steps
Any free-text or photo-based description from homeowner
agents.main_job: Turn whatever the homeowner gives us (text + photos) into a clean, structured project record that works with the current DB and estimating flows.
typical_actions: Suggest default values for rooms, project type, and square footage based on what homeowner wrote.
Auto-fill structured fields (rooms, interior/exterior, addition vs alteration).
Highlight possible mechanical/electrical/plumbing needs for the estimator dashboard.
Save back into existing project fields instead of creating new tables where possible.

agents.name: Smart Estimate Companion Agent
where_it_runs: After intake is done but before estimator is scheduled
On a 'Cost Preview' or 'What should I expect?' screen
agents.main_job: Give the homeowner a friendly cost range and explainers based on what we already know about the project.
uses_existing_things: any existing internal pricing logic or estimate templates
typical_actions: Output low / medium / high cost ranges (even if rough).
Explain what drives cost up or down in plain English.
Recommend add-ons (3D design, architectural plans) using current Stripe products / upsell logic.
Suggest time windows to pass into the estimator scheduling flow that already exists.

agents.name: Smart Homeowner Support Agent
where_it_runs: Homeowner portal chat or help widget
FAQ / support pages linked to logged-in users
agents.main_job: Answer status and process questions using existing project, scheduling, and payment data.
typical_actions: Answer 'Where is my project in the process?' using workflow_status.
Tell them if an estimator is scheduled and show date/time.
Summarize bid status in simple language (X bids received, Y shortlisted).
Escalate to human support when unsure and log conversation for admins.

categories.name: Estimator Agents
categories.description: Agents that support estimators with scheduling, walkthroughs, and estimate creation, using the existing Estimator Portal and DB.

agents.name: Smart Scheduling & Routing Agent
where_it_runs: Immediately after Stripe confirms estimate payment
Inside existing estimator scheduling edge function / n8n flow
agents.main_job: Pick the best estimator and schedule the visit using current logic (ZIP, availability, travel buffer).
uses_existing_things: existing Google Calendar integration and event creation code
typical_actions: Auto-select estimator instead of manual assignment when possible.
Compute start/end times using current travel buffer rules.
Create calendar event with Maps link and push notifications (SMS/email) through existing flows.
Expose suggestions to admins if auto-assign fails, instead of silently erroring.

agents.name: Smart Walkthrough Agent
where_it_runs: Estimator dashboard during a site visit
Walkthrough / photos / notes tab
agents.main_job: Turn estimator's photos + notes into a structured scope that drops cleanly into the existing estimate workflow.
typical_actions: Group photos by room and tag them.
Extract bullets from free-text notes (demo, framing, MEP, finishes).
Pre-populate scope/line items in whatever format the current estimating UI expects.
Flag red flags (structural, water damage, etc.) as notes for internal use.

agents.name: SmartEstimate Agent
where_it_runs: Estimator creates or edits an estimate
Admin converts walkthrough into proposal
agents.main_job: Generate or improve the estimate using the existing pricing model, not replace it.
uses_existing_things: any existing templates or libraries for costs
typical_actions: Suggest line items and quantities from the structured scope.
Fill in rough pricing where allowed, using current defaults.
Insert standard disclaimers already used by the business.
Produce a homeowner-facing summary and a detailed contractor/RFP version using the existing PDF / document flow.

categories.name: Contractor Agents
categories.description: Agents that live inside the Contractor Portal and help contractors respond to RFPs and manage work.

agents.name: Bid Preparation Agent
where_it_runs: When a contractor opens an RFP detail page
On a 'Generate Draft Bid' button
agents.main_job: Pre-fill a bid for the contractor based on estimator scope and photos, keeping the current bid structure.
typical_actions: Summarize the job in 3–5 bullets for the contractor.
Pre-fill line items with quantities where possible.
Offer low / mid / high suggested pricing for internal reference only.
Remind contractor about common exclusions and terms used today.

agents.name: Smart Contractor Workflow Agent
where_it_runs: Contractor dashboard (list of active jobs)
Notifications panel
agents.main_job: Keep contractors aware of next steps and upcoming tasks based on current timelines and messages.
typical_actions: Generate today / this week task lists per project.
Suggest update messages to homeowners when something changes.
Highlight stalled jobs (no update in X days).

categories.name: Subcontractor Agents
categories.description: Agents that help subs price and align their schedule with the GC's plan.

agents.name: Sub Bid Agent
where_it_runs: Subcontractor bid invitations sent from Project Manager or Contractor view
agents.main_job: Break the GC's scope into trade-specific pieces and help the sub price and pick dates.
typical_actions: Show only the relevant sections of scope for that trade.
Offer date windows that fit the master timeline.
Prepare a draft bid the sub can edit.

categories.name: PM / Coordinator Agents
categories.description: Agents that help build timelines and manage communication using the existing Admin / PM tools.

agents.name: Smart Timeline Agent
where_it_runs: Admin / PM dashboard once a project is 'approved' or 'contractor selected'
agents.main_job: Propose a project timeline that uses the existing scheduling and milestone structure.
uses_existing_things: current milestone model or status pipeline
material lead time fields if present
typical_actions: Generate a first draft Gantt-style plan (phases, dates).
Mark critical path tasks and suggested buffer days.
Update existing timeline records instead of creating a new system.

agents.name: Smart Communication Agent
where_it_runs: PM / Coordinator messaging areas
Any place admins send bulk or templated updates
agents.main_job: Draft clear, friendly updates and requests based on actual project state.
typical_actions: Write delay explanations tied to real causes (permits, materials, weather).
Draft meeting requests with suggested date/times.
Create clean, non-technical homeowner updates PMs can approve.

categories.name: Admin + Compliance Agents
categories.description: Agents that help SmartReno staff with QA, permits, and warranty work using existing back-office tools.

agents.name: Smart QA Agent
where_it_runs: Admin review screens for estimates, RFPs, and bids
agents.main_job: Flag issues and missing pieces before anything is sent to homeowners or contractors.
uses_existing_things: current business rules (labor rates, markup ranges, disclaimers)
typical_actions: Flag missing rooms or big mismatches between intake and estimate.
Warn if labor or margin is outside normal ranges.
Check that standard disclaimers are present.
Produce a short 'QA checklist' for the admin to approve.

agents.name: Smart Permit Agent
where_it_runs: Admin 'Permit' tab you're building with NJ municipalities and forms
agents.main_job: Help the team understand what forms and contacts are needed for a given job and town.
uses_existing_things: stored PDF templates or online forms
typical_actions: List which forms are required based on project type.
Surface contact info and links for that municipality.
Draft email templates to send to building departments.
Eventually suggest how to fill-in forms based on existing project data.

agents.name: Smart Warranty Agent
where_it_runs: Warranty claim portal / admin dashboard
agents.main_job: Sort, triage, and route warranty claims to the right contractor or team.
typical_actions: Classify claim type (leak, finish issue, mechanical, etc.).
Check if the issue appears covered by current warranty rules.
Suggest the responsible party and next steps.
Generate a short internal summary plus a homeowner-facing acknowledgement message.
